title: cochlidium paucinervatum
intro: Cochlidium paucinervatum, also known as the few-nerved cochlidium, is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a type of tropical fern native to the Philippines, known for its medicinal properties and health benefits. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Cochlidium paucinervatum is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and its extracts are also used in herbal supplements, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
